informal: Let $P$ be a normal Sylow $p$-subgroup of $G$ and let $H$ be any subgroup of $G$. Prove that $P \cap H$ is the unique Sylow $p$-subgroup of $H$.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_33 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] {p : ℕ}
  (P : Sylow p G) [hP : P.Normal] (H : Subgroup G) [Fintype H] :
  (∀ R : Sylow p H, R.toSubgroup = (H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H) ∧
  Nonempty (Sylow p H)
reasoning: The informal claim "P∩H is the unique Sylow p-subgroup of H" decomposes into existence (there is a Sylow p-subgroup of H, and it equals P∩H) and uniqueness (every Sylow p-subgroup of H equals P∩H). The Lean statement encodes uniqueness via `∀ R : Sylow p H, R.toSubgroup = (H ⊓ P).subgroupOf H` and existence via `Nonempty (Sylow p H)`. Together with the existence clause, the universal clause forces P∩H itself to be a Sylow p-subgroup of H, and the universal clause then guarantees uniqueness. The hypotheses (G finite, P a normal Sylow p-subgroup, H an arbitrary subgroup) match the informal statement exactly, and `(H ⊓ P).subgroupOf H` is the standard Mathlib encoding of P∩H viewed as a subgroup of H. No extra or missing hypotheses are introduced.
